Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest 2 from trevis-108vm9: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/507b264b-aabe-435e-a823-dba8d7180333 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Hi James,

Thanks for the review.
I was thinking to define a new callback lnd_nl_alloc to allow custom lnd parameters without modifying the ioctl interface. But this is a in-depth change, we have to save the size of tunable and ni_lnd_tunables should be change to a pointer.

What do you think of it?

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

If you look at lnet_fill_ni_info() their is a section to test tun size to see if it changed for the API. I believe when I wrote that years ago it would handle ever increasing tunables for ioctl handling.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

commit.subject: LU-12452 lnet: inherit default lnd tunings from modparams
commit.message:
LU-12452 lnet: inherit default lnd tunings from modparams

When a network is added dynamically (via Netlink), LNet assumes that
all the "unset" or default LND parameters are 0. But for some
use cases, 0 could be a valid value.

This patch modifies the callback lnet_lnd.lnd_nl_set() to set default
values: a NULL attr argument means "set the default value".

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Ifb91ae63d96131ed87d9fae7d91b8b18df4c9ce9
